Hey, what's going on everyone? Okay, this is phase one of the hybrid process. Okay, so I like having visuals to a company of concepts because I think it helps everyone understand things better. So whatever you're doing for your old model or however you're currently making money is kind of the old model on the left, right? And so the easiest way to transition is to not actually break that yet, but actually to increase your certainty that when that time comes, you'll be able to do it. And the fastest way to do that is to basically build up your side hustle income, which is what the hybrid model will do for you. As you can see in the second scenario on the right hand side, over here, that your existing business doesn't really change. And so that means that you don't have to have any conversion process, conversations with any of your clients or change expectations or any of that stuff, but you're simply marketing to cold traffic and selling them into your new hybrid model, which has significantly lower overhead and significantly higher upside in profit. And so my theory on the concept of the word side hustle is that the only reason people do side hustles is because their dollars per hour on their side hustle is higher than their dollar per hour on their main hustle. And the issue that the reason their side hustle is not their main hustle is that they just can't get it big enough to make as much as they make on their main hustle in terms of volume, but not in terms of profit or dollars per hour. And so the goal is to basically make your side hustle your main hustle. And so with this program, this process, we will be able to do that, but we're going to start it this way so that we just decrease risk on your side and just not overwhelm you. And so this is what it looks like in terms of a process standpoint. All we are doing here is just running ads and we're selling people into hybrid, which is remote. All right, and that means that they're coming in for their six week front end program. And then we're going to transition them into a 12 month program at $77 week. And that is it. And that's why it says 200 to 400 a month because depending on how much you trust me in terms of your pricing, I would recommend you do 77 a week as your downsell from 99 a week, which is what they did in their first six weeks with you. And so here's an example of Dylan, who did exactly this. You can see him underneath his sister quick update I've been on vacation most this month and managed to sell 25 hybrid clients at 800. So he sold it at 800 first for six weeks, you don't need to do that. I recommend you start at 599. And that's just the front end. You know, this is one of the easiest sales ever, it's a 90% were laid down sales. This new model is going to change the game, etc. You know, can't can't wait. Right. And so essentially kept these gym the same. And then he added hybrid on top of his business. Okay. And so he had 90 semi private clients at 450 a month. And then he met with 30 prospects, exit like from advertising, and he sold 25 for the many hundreds, which means he added $20,000 with the audit cost. That's it. Okay. And then you meet with those people to sell them into the long term recurring at the half of them. Okay. So he had 100% profit and outside business on top of his gym. And so just as a side note, Dylan has a very small studio and so he's always had capacity issues and now those are no longer an issue or cap on his income. And if you're in the same situation, if you're sub leasing or you have a smaller facility and capacity has always been the problem, that's no longer the case with this because the only thing you need to do is meet with somebody. And that doesn't require much space at all. Okay. And so this is kind of phase one of the process is that we're going to build up the side income, not just from the front end sales front end sales are great, but what we want is we want to build up that recurring. Right. And so once we get that recurring to 50 clients, or more at 30, 331 per month, I think is how it works out. It's about 15 $16,000 a month recurring that's coming from your, from your hybrid training. And then we stack the front end revenue on top of that if you're selling 30 a month, then you now you're at 30, 35,000 at, you know, ish per month. And at that point, you'll be able to make a very strong case to your existing clients that they should transition to this new thing, etc. Okay. And that'll kind of bring us to phase two. But I just like to show some visuals to kind of break down a little bit more. Because sometimes words and camera can be can be difficult to copy. But this is phase one. This is all we're doing. Do not pass phase one until you've hit those benchmarks until you have 50 clients on recurring, not 50 clients sold, but 50 clients on recurring revenue in your gym that have made it come into staying to you, staying with you for an extended period of time, six months, 12 months, etc. All right.
